message.content:
Research the LATEST QUARTERLY RESULTS and MARKET DATA of Vinyas Innovative Technologies Ltd (NSE: VINNY), an Indian EMS/defense-electronics company in Mysore. Indian fiscal years end March; report in INR crore. Today is June 2026.

Use WebSearch and WebFetch on screener.in (try https://www.screener.in/company/VINNY/), moneycontrol.com, tickertape.in, nseindia.com, BSE.

Gather:
1. Most recent quarterly results available (likely Q4 FY25 / quarter ending Mar 2025, or any 2025/2026 quarter): revenue, EBITDA, net profit/PAT, and YoY growth % vs same quarter prior year. List the last 4 quarters if shown.
2. Shares outstanding (number of shares / equity capital)
3. Current market capitalization (INR crore) - note the date
4. Current share price (INR) - note the exact date
5. Dividend history: any dividends declared? dividend per share, dividend yield, payout ratio. Note years.
6. 52-week high/low, P/E ratio, book value if available.

For EACH number give the source URL and date. Flag conflicts. Quote exact numbers. Note that market data changes daily - capture the date of any price/mcap.

Return structured markdown. Include all source URLs with dates. Do NOT write files. Return findings as text output only.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

## Consolidated Financial Results - FY26 (Full Year)

Vinyas Innovative Technologies reported a 58.92% YoY increase in FY26 net profit to ₹30.87 crore, driven by a 29.45% rise in revenue to ₹514.32 crore.

EBITDA rose 46% to ₹64.77 crore, with margins improving to 12.5% from 11.09% in the previous year.

## H2 FY26 (Second Half) Performance

For the half-year ended March 31, 2026, the company recorded a net profit of ₹21.53 crore on revenue of ₹303.59 crore.

## Order Book and Growth Indicators

The company's order book stands at ₹1,309.06 crore as of March 31, 2026, providing 5-6 years of visibility, while total order wins for the year reached ₹960.38 crore.

## Additional Highlights

The company achieved NADCAP accreditation, raised ₹150 crore in capital, and is expanding its system integration capabilities.

content.text:
I have all the data needed. The company reports half-yearly (not quarterly), which explains the screener "quarterly" columns being 6-month periods. Let me confirm the H1 FY26 figures to complete the half-year picture (H1 = FY26 total minus H2).

H1 FY26 = Revenue 514.32 − 303.59 ≈ ₹210.73 Cr; PAT 30.87 − 21.53 = ₹9.34 Cr (matches the "Sep 2025: Sales ₹212 Cr, PAT ₹9 Cr" screener column). The data is internally consistent. I have enough to report.

---

# Vinyas Innovative Technologies Ltd (NSE: VINYAS, BSE: 542323)

EMS / defense-electronics company, Mysore. Figures in INR crore. Compiled 15 June 2026.

## Important ticker / data-quality notes
- The correct NSE symbol is **VINYAS**, not VINNY. The URL `screener.in/company/VINNY/` returns a *different, unrelated company* ("Vinny Overseas Ltd") — do not use it. The correct page is **https://www.screener.in/company/VINYAS/**.
- **Vinyas reports HALF-YEARLY, not quarterly.** It is not required to publish full quarterly statements, so there is no standalone "Q4" figure. Screener's "quarterly" table columns are actually 6-month (H1/H2) periods. Several aggregator sites mislabel the **FY25 full-year** revenue (₹396.64 / ₹400.15 Cr) as "Q4 FY25" — that is a labeling error, not a quarter.
- Latest reported period is **FY26 (year ended 31 Mar 2026)**, results released ~mid-May 2026.

## 1. Latest results (FY26, year ended Mar 2026) — consolidated

| Metric | FY26 (Mar 2026) | FY25 (Mar 2025) | YoY growth |
|---|---|---|---|
| Revenue from operations | **₹514.32 Cr** | ₹400.15 Cr | **+29.45%** |
| EBITDA | **₹64.77 Cr** (margin 12.5%) | ~₹44.4 Cr (margin 11.09%) | **+46%** |
| PAT (net profit) | **₹30.87 Cr** (margin ~6.0%) | ₹19.42 Cr | **+58.9%** |

Source: scanx.trade (FY26 results report), Yahoo Finance FY26 earnings-call highlights, Avinash Gorakshakar (X / company IR summary). FY25 comparatives: stockanalysis.com, business-standard.

### Half-yearly breakdown (the most granular periods Vinyas reports)

| Period | Revenue | EBITDA/Op. profit | PAT |
|---|---|---|---|
| H2 FY26 (Oct 2025–Mar 2026) | **₹303.59 Cr** | ~₹40 Cr (screener) | **₹21.53 Cr** |
| H1 FY26 (Apr–Sep 2025) | ~₹210.73 Cr (screener: ₹212 Cr) | ~₹21 Cr | ~₹9.34 Cr (screener: ₹9 Cr) |
| H2 FY25 (Oct 2024–Mar 2025) | ₹247 Cr (screener) | ₹24 Cr | ₹12 Cr |
| H1 FY25 (Apr–Sep 2024) | ₹150 Cr (screener) | ₹17 Cr | ₹7 Cr |

H2 FY26 YoY (vs H2 FY25): revenue ₹303.59 Cr vs ₹247 Cr ≈ **+23%**; PAT ₹21.53 Cr vs ₹12 Cr ≈ **+79%**.
Source: scanx.trade (H2 FY26: revenue ₹303.59 Cr, PAT ₹21.53 Cr); screener.in/company/VINYAS/ (half-year columns). Figures reconcile (H1 = FY26 − H2).

Other FY26 disclosures: order book **₹1,309.06 Cr** as of 31 Mar 2026 (5–6 yrs visibility); order wins ₹960.38 Cr in FY26; raised ₹150 Cr via preferential equity; received NADCAP accreditation. Source: scanx.trade, Yahoo Finance earnings call.

## 2. Shares outstanding / equity capital
- Shares outstanding: **~1.258 crore (12.58 million)** — finology.in ("1.26 Cr"), stockanalysis.com ("12.58M"), 15 Jun 2026.
- Equity capital: **₹13 Cr**; face value **₹10/share** (so ~1.26 cr shares). Source: screener.in/company/VINYAS/.
- Note: share count reflects the recent ₹150 Cr preferential capital raise.

## 3. Market capitalization
- **₹1,768 Cr** — screener.in & finology.in, as of 15 Jun 2026.
- **₹1,840.52 Cr** — tickertape.in / business-standard, quoted "as of 12 Jun 2026."
- Conflict driven by date and share-count assumptions; current (15 Jun) figure ~₹1,768 Cr (price ₹1,404.95 × ~1.258 cr shares ≈ ₹1,767 Cr).

## 4. Current share price
- **₹1,404.95**, as of **15 Jun 2026, 3:29 PM IST** (down ~3.94% on the day). Source: tickertape.in, stockanalysis.com, finology.in. Intraday range ₹1,421.05–₹1,524 / day avg ₹1,472.53 per business-standard.

## 5. Dividend history
- **No dividends.** Dividend yield **0.00%**, dividend payout **0%**. The company has not paid dividends (profits retained for growth/capex). Source: screener.in/company/VINYAS/, tickertape.in, finology.in, simplywall.st. No DPS to report.

## 6. Valuation / ratios (as of 15 Jun 2026)
- **52-week high/low: ₹1,524 / ₹809.** Source: tickertape.in, screener.in, finology.in.
- **P/E ratio:** screener **57.3**; tickertape **59.62**; stockanalysis **60.04** (differences from EPS-source timing). EPS (TTM) ~₹24.5.
- **Book value:** **₹187 / share** (screener, finology, stockanalysis ₹187.31).
- **P/B:** screener **8.0x**; stockanalysis **7.81x**; tickertape lists **12.52** (outlier — likely stale or different basis; treat ~7.8–8.0x as correct).
- ROE **~14–16%** (FY25 ~14.1%, "last year" 16.1% per screener); ROCE **~16–19%**. 5-yr profit CAGR ~90.8%.

## Conflicts flagged
1. **"Q4 FY25" mislabel:** ₹396.64 Cr revenue / ₹19.42 Cr PAT quoted by tickertape/business-standard as "Q4 FY25" is actually the **FY25 full year**. Vinyas does not report quarterly.
2. **Market cap:** ₹1,768 Cr (15 Jun) vs ₹1,840.52 Cr (12 Jun) — date-driven.
3. **P/B:** tickertape's 12.52 conflicts with ~7.8–8.0x elsewhere; tickertape value appears erroneous.
4. **FY26 revenue rounding:** ₹514.32 Cr (precise, scanx/IR) vs "~₹518 Cr" (Yahoo earnings-call transcript rounding). Use ₹514.32 Cr.

## Sources (with dates accessed 15 Jun 2026)
- [screener.in/company/VINYAS](https://www.screener.in/company/VINYAS/) — financials, ratios, half-year table, no-dividend
- [scanx.trade — Vinyas FY26 net profit ₹30.87 Cr](https://scanx.trade/stock-market-news/companies/vinyas-innovative-technologies-reports-fy26-net-profit-of-3-086-83-lakh/41584718) — FY26 & H2 FY26 results
- [Yahoo Finance — Vinyas FY26 earnings-call highlights](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ltd-nse-010216958.html) — EBITDA, order book, ₹150 Cr raise
- [tickertape.in/stocks/vinyas-innovative-technologies-VINYA](https://www.tickertape.in/stocks/vinyas-innovative-technologies-VINYA) — price, mcap, P/E, 52-wk
- [stockanalysis.com/quote/nse/VINYAS/statistics](https://stockanalysis.com/quote/nse/VINYAS/statistics/) — shares o/s, price, book value, P/E
- [ticker.finology.in/company/VINYAS](https://ticker.finology.in/company/VINYAS) — shares o/s, mcap, ratios
- [business-standard.com — VINYAS share price](https://www.business-standard.com/markets/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ltd-share-price-88617.html) — intraday price
- [Avinash Gorakshakar / X — FY26 IR summary](https://x.com/AvinashGoraksha/status/2060233728673321080) — revenue +29% to ₹514.32 Cr, EBITDA ₹64.77 Cr, PAT +59%

**Bottom line:** Latest results are **FY26 (Mar 2026)**: Revenue **₹514.32 Cr** (+29.5% YoY), EBITDA **₹64.77 Cr** (+46%), PAT **₹30.87 Cr** (+58.9%). Stock ₹1,404.95 (15 Jun 2026), market cap ~₹1,768 Cr, ~1.26 cr shares, P/E ~57–60, book value ₹187, 52-wk ₹809–1,524, **no dividends**. The company reports half-yearly, not quarterly — there is no true standalone "Q4."
